name: Nantucket Cioppino
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Carrots, peeled and coarsely chopped
3 cups Coarsely chopped green bell pepper
8 cups Peeled and coarsely chopped tomatoes
2 each 28 oz cans plum tomatoes with basil, chopped
2 cups Dry red wine OR 1 cup dry red and 1 cup marsala
1 1/2 cups Finely chopped fresh Italian parsley
recipeInstructions:
1. In a very large kettle, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
2. Add the carrots, onion, bell pepper, and garlic; cook, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes.
3. Add the mushrooms, tomatoes (with juice), tomato paste, wine, broth, lemon slices, half of the parsley, basil, oregano, and salt and pepper to taste.
4. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes.
5. Stir in the swordfish and lobster pieces; cover and simmer for an additional 15 minutes.
6. Add the clams, mussels, cod, and shrimp; cover and simmer for another 10 minutes, or until the clams and mussels have opened and the fish is cooked.
7. Serve the cioppino in large bowls, sprinkling with any remaining parsley.
